title: Test OOF beta or Venn-Abers calibration only as a blend stabilizer
summary: Calibration usually does not improve a single model’s ROC AUC because monotone transforms preserve ranking, but it can materially help when combining heterogeneous specialists.
rationale: Calibration usually does not improve a single model’s ROC AUC because monotone transforms preserve ranking, but it can materially help when combining heterogeneous specialists. Beta calibration is a low-variance alternative to Platt/isotonic, and out-of-bag Venn-style calibration has evidence of tighter, more reliable probabilities than using a separate small calibration split.
implementation_hint: Generate leak-free OOF predictions under grouped race-level folds, then compare three ensemble inputs: raw ranks, beta-calibrated probabilities, and Venn-Abers-style calibrated probabilities. Select by blend CV stability and public-LB variance, not by standalone calibrated-model AUC alone.
expected_effect: Most likely gain is a more stable ensemble and less fold-sensitive weighting, rather than a large boost from any one calibrated model.
risk: If the OOF protocol is not strictly grouped by race/year, calibration will look better than it is and contaminate ensemble selection.
